_metadata.description: PharmaGuide public six-pillar Quality Score (decision score). PHASE 1 = side-by-side scaffold: pillars are LINEAR remaps of the existing v4 module dimensions/bonuses into the 20/20/20/15/15/10 frame. This intentionally reproduces the raw inversion (e.g. creatine ~87) so the bias is visible; category-aware pillar adapters land in later PRs. raw_score_v4_100 (the audit/math score) is NEVER changed.

formulation_subscale._doc: PR4: category-aware purpose-fit formulation. The raw formulation dimension is scored out of a breadth-dependent max (30 for generic/sports) that a SINGLE ingredient structurally cannot reach (A2-A5 = premium-form stacking, synergy, absorption enhancers are multi-ingredient features). Normalize each archetype to its ACHIEVABLE form ceiling so a best-in-class product earns ~19-20 by being the OPTIMAL FORM, not by faking breadth. Computed from the real form-quality components, so a cheap form (magnesium oxide, low A1) still scores low. This is purpose-fit, not premium-form dominance.

verification_subscale._doc: PR2: hard third-party signals saturate the cap (over-provisioned, internal sum clamps at 15) so a SUBSET (e.g. one gold cert) maxes it. Self-asserted cGMP is table stakes (near-zero). FAIL OPEN: a product with no third-party cert AND no COA is data-UNKNOWN, not uncertified — it floors at a neutral baseline so the ~62% of the catalog we lack cert data on is not cratered (confidence carries the gap). Soft reputation/region capped at 3; physician/sustainability dropped. The wall truly lifts only when real cert data is sourced (cert_registry task).
